# UC-17.1.64 · ISE Custom Posture Provisioning (CPP) Install and Update Audit

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Operations, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Before a computer can be checked for compliance, a small program must be installed on it. We watch how often that installation fails so we know when many users can't get the program.*

---

## Description

Tracks Cisco Custom Posture Provisioning (CPP) installation and update flows; detects high failure rates that suggest installer or signing-cert issues.

## Value

Endpoints can't run posture checks without CPP installed. Install failures break the entire posture chain for affected endpoints.

## Implementation

Schedule the search every 1 hour over the last 4 hours; trigger on count > 0; throttle on `policy_name` for 1 hour.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Break the agent download server. Many install failures. UC-17.1.64 fires.

### Negative scenario

Restore. Fail rate drops.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (15080, 15081, 15082, 15083, 15084, 15085)
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode IN ("15082","15085"))) as failures by posture_policy
| where failures > 10
```

## Visualization

Per-policy install/failure trend.

## Known False Positives

- Initial rollout — high install failures during the first day.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Posture Agent Provisioning](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_compliance_segmentation.html)
